section_miscategorizations.text: The patient denies numbness in his fingers.
section_miscategorizations.reason: This is a patient-reported symptom review (negative neurologic symptom) rather than an objective exam, lab, or imaging finding.

section_miscategorizations.text: The patient is experiencing shoulder pain and pain in the left hand.
section_miscategorizations.reason: This is a restatement of symptoms rather than a diagnostic impression or problem list; no left hand pain was documented in HPI or findings, so its appearance only in Assessment is misleading.

section_miscategorizations.text: He needs to have this fracture healed.
section_miscategorizations.reason: This implies the presence of a fracture (a diagnostic/imaging-based finding) that is not supported by the documented history or exam; if a fracture were known, it should be documented as an objective finding or clearly as a diagnosis (e.g., 'left shoulder fracture'), not as a vague need statement.
